acceptance_targets: [CH18_SIGHT_RULE_COLLAPSE] Pass if a cold reader can read snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:119, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:169, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:203, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:241 in the revision and state exactly what Maren can and cannot see without contradiction. Fail if the chapter still both grants him direct visual perception of the yard or contamination and states that he sees nothing at all.

[CH18_STYLE_BIBLE_ECHO] Pass if a reader comparing the revised chapter against outline/style_bible.json finds no near-verbatim clause and no obviously copied beat-shape at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:79, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:171, or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:245. Thessa must still assign Barren Rill and Aldric must still correct Maren's direction, but the language must be unmistakably new.

[CH18_OVERSTATEMENT_AFTER_ARRIVAL] Pass if a cold reader can infer from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:97 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:109 and from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:253 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:259 that Thessa now treats Maren as a colleague and that he will continue the work, without the revision explicitly restating those meanings in abstract thesis language. Fail if the revised passages still explain the beat after it has already landed on the page.

[CH18_PROSE_TEMPLATE_RECURRENCE] Pass if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:53, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:69, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:163, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:243 contain at most one total use of "specific" as an intensifier and do not stack back-to-back paragraphs on the same "the way ..." or explanatory-comparison scaffold. Fail if those spans still read as variations of the same sentence template.
[R-04] Ch 18 contains no more than 1 occurrence of 'eleven years.' The retained instance appears in the final pages as a deliberate thematic bookend.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can read snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:119, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:169, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:203, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:241 in the revision and state exactly what Maren can and cannot see without contradiction. Fail if the chapter still both grants him direct visual perception of the yard or contamination and states that he sees nothing at all.
findings.problem: The chapter cannot keep Maren's visual capability straight. It gives him direct visual access to the contamination shimmer and the girl's wave, then states flatly that he has always been blind and cannot see anything. Because the style bible locks narration to Maren's sensory limitations, this reads as both a continuity contradiction and a POV-control break.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the visual-access beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:119 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:241 together with the blindness assertions at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:169 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:203. Choose one rule and enforce it everywhere in the chapter: if Maren is fully blind, recast those moments through sound, touch, smell, or Aldric's mediation; if he can perceive raw quest-light or other visual data, rewrite the blindness language so the chapter no longer claims total sightlessness.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader comparing the revised chapter against outline/style_bible.json finds no near-verbatim clause and no obviously copied beat-shape at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:79, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:171, or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:245. Thessa must still assign Barren Rill and Aldric must still correct Maren's direction, but the language must be unmistakably new.
findings.problem: The chapter lifts calibration-only style-bible material into finished scene language. Thessa's assignment echoes the example built around a farmstead twelve miles east with children, the remembered training quote repeats "Slower this time," and Aldric's closing direction beat mirrors the calibration "Left ... your left" exchange. Reusing example-line phrasing in chapter-defining beats damages voice integrity and makes the scene feel assembled from notes instead of spoken by these characters in the moment.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the assignment exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:79, the remembered training quote at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:171, and the closing direction beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:245 so they keep the same plot function and character relationship but use fresh phrasing and a fresh conversational shape rather than the style-bible calibration wording.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can infer from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:97 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:109 and from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:253 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:259 that Thessa now treats Maren as a colleague and that he will continue the work, without the revision explicitly restating those meanings in abstract thesis language. Fail if the revised passages still explain the beat after it has already landed on the page.
findings.problem: The chapter lands its key turns in scene and then immediately translates them into thesis language. Thessa's "Go eat it" plus Aldric's "That was an acknowledgment" already establish the professional recognition, but the narration then explains that she has treated Maren as a colleague. The final image of him already walking toward the next job is enough, yet the coda re-proves the point with abstract statements about why he keeps going and how he is now the one who answers. That extra paraphrase weakens the ending's force.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or recast the explanatory aftermath at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:107 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:255 while preserving the must-land beats from the scene. Keep Thessa's cold professional acknowledgment and keep Maren leaving Barren Rill committed to the next site, but let action, image, or one earned line carry those meanings instead of narrator certification.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:53, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:69, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:163, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:243 contain at most one total use of "specific" as an intensifier and do not stack back-to-back paragraphs on the same "the way ..." or explanatory-comparison scaffold. Fail if those spans still read as variations of the same sentence template.
findings.problem: The prose falls into a repeated workshoped template: "the specific ... of someone," "the way ...," and explanatory simile wrappers recur across Derry, Thessa, the watching family, Aldric's changed tone, and the closing road paragraph. Because the chapter keeps reaching for the same interpretive sentence machinery, the promised specificity starts sounding mechanical rather than freshly observed. This also looks like over-literal reuse of style-bible token habits instead of live sentence-making.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:53, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:69, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:81, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:163, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:171, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:181, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_18.md:243, replace several of the repeated scaffolds with direct observation, action, or a different sentence shape. Keep the content, but stop defaulting to "specific," "the way," and comparison-led explanation every time the narration interprets a person or beat.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 18 contains no more than 1 occurrence of 'eleven years.' The retained instance appears in the final pages as a deliberate thematic bookend.
findings.evidence: The phrase 'eleven years' appears 7 times in Ch 18 (lines 4660-4918), the final chapter. By this point the reader has encountered the phrase 82+ times. Examples: L4794 'the same evaluative look people had given him for eleven years'; L4822 'every family had watched him for eleven years'; L4824 'For eleven years, he'd taken their money and left'; L4886 'every family had asked for eleven years'; L4914 'he'd spent eleven years not doing this.'
findings.problem: The final chapter re-proves the fraud duration 7 times after 82+ prior occurrences. L4822, L4824, L4886, and L4914 all restate the same fact within 92 lines. The ending should demonstrate transformation through action, not by restating the baseline. First established Ch 1 L9.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'eleven years' in Ch 18 from 7 to at most 1. The single strongest use should be the final thematic turn (L4914 is the best candidate). Cut or rephrase all other instances. The final chapter earns its power from what Maren does, not from how many times we are reminded what he did not do.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel must commit fully to its own weirdness. A System-blind con man with a dead paladin and a dead assassin arguing in his skull is inherently absurd, and the novel should let that absurdity live alongside genuine grief, genuine violence, and genuine mystery without apologizing for the tonal range. The darkest moments and the funniest moments may occupy the same scene. When they do, let both registers operate simultaneously — do not sacrifice comedy for gravitas or gravitas for comedy. Risk means specificity: the specific sensation of a body doing things you did not tell it to do, the specific comedy of a dead paladin critiquing your hygiene while you're running for your life, the specific horror of carrying incomplete people whose memories have holes, the specific shame of learning that your eleven years of fraud may have killed people. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. The novel's emotional range should be wider than any single genre label would suggest.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in the narration of violence, bodily experience, moral compromise, or physical consequence is a draft-level failure. Maren is a con man who has seen hard things and done dirty work in poor villages for eleven years — his narration should not flinch where he would not flinch. The style bible's sensory bias toward tactile and proprioceptive detail means physical consequence must be rendered through the body: what it feels like to throw up, to kill someone, to have your muscles operate without your consent, to perform a ritual that requires you to open yourself to contamination. Summary and abstraction at the moment of consequence is always worse than presence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Aldric, whose low contraction level is a deliberate character marker that should erode over the course of the novel. Uncontracted forms in other characters' speech are reserved for emphasis, deliberation, or ceremony. In a world of practical people doing unglamorous work, stiff diction is the exception.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate between hot and quiet chapters as a structural principle. No two consecutive chapters should open the same way — if one opens with action, the next should open with interiority or setting; if one opens with dialogue, the next should open with physical sensation or observation. Vary scene count between chapters: compressed single-scene chapters after multi-scene ones. Let quiet chapters stay quiet — do not insert action beats or revelations to maintain pace. The pacing engine is Maren's voice and the interplay between the three internal consciousnesses, not external plot pressure.
prose_style_profile.diction: Colloquial with earned elevation. Default register is smart but not literary — a con man's English, precise about people and vague about feelings. Elevation reserved for moments of genuine awe, grief, or the rare glimpse of something beautiful. Fantasy-register portentousness is a draft-level failure — no 'ancient darkness' or 'powers beyond comprehension.' The world is weird and dangerous and ordinary all at once, and the prose should match.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Sentence fragments imitating action-movie or thriller prose — subject-dropping and article-stripping for false urgency
Semicolon-heavy scholarly narration that distances the reader from embodied experience
Present-tense micro-shifts for intensity — the novel is past tense throughout, no exceptions
Filtering words ('he saw,' 'he felt,' 'he noticed,' 'he realized') when direct rendering is available
Fantasy-voice portentousness: 'ancient,' 'dark power,' 'something stirred,' 'a presence beyond reckoning'
Comic narration that winks at the reader or breaks the fourth wall instead of committing to the situation
Repeated metaphor templates — if a comparison structure appears twice in a chapter, the second instance is a tic
Emotional summary replacing scene presence: 'He was overwhelmed by grief' instead of rendering what grief looks and feels like in the body
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Maren throughout. Aldric and Vess are experienced as internal voices — they speak as italicized dialogue within Maren's consciousness, clearly distinguished from his own thoughts. Maren cannot access their thoughts directly; he hears what they say to him, not what they think. The narration inherits Maren's sensory limitations: the System interface is always mediated through Aldric or Vess's descriptions, never rendered directly until the single exception in Chapter 17.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by emotional register. Default: medium-length sentences with periodic short declaratives for grounding. When Maren is performing (lying, charming, faking): smoother, more controlled rhythm with longer subordinate-clause chains. When Maren is honest or overwhelmed: choppier, more fragmented, more concrete. Fight scenes: visceral and slightly disoriented, reflecting Maren's System-blindness — sensation and proprioception without the visual data everyone else has. Quiet scenes: allow longer, more observational sentences. The rhythm should breathe differently in every chapter.
sensory_bias: tactile — Maren's primary sense channel; the physical world is how he navigates System-blindness
auditory — the hum of System activity, Aldric and Vess's voices, the specific sounds of quest-light and contamination
proprioceptive — Maren's body doing things he didn't choose; the internal sensation of carrying fragments; the physical cost of consumption
olfactory — contamination has smell; ritual has smell; the world stinks in specific ways that the System-sighted don't notice because they navigate by interface
